_comment: OPTIONAL. Parser drift is environment-specific (always-raw infra parsers create false positives). Do NOT deploy by default. Tune the drift_ratio threshold (>=0.05) and confirm which parsers are legitimately raw before enabling. Deploy only after tuning; create Disabled and enable when validated.

data.name: Ingest Health - Parser Drift (optional, tune first)
data.description: A parser normally normalising is now emitting events without dataSource.name/category at a rate >= 5%. OPTIONAL: needs per-environment tuning (raw-by-design parsers cause false positives). Deploy Disabled, tune drift_ratio, then enable.

scheduledParams.query: parser=* | lookup ih_excl_n = reason from ingestHealthExclusions.csv by value =:anycase dataSource.name | lookup ih_excl_v = reason from ingestHealthExclusions.csv by value =:anycase dataSource.vendor | filter (ih_excl_n = null and ih_excl_v = null) | let is_unparsed = ((dataSource.name && dataSource.category) ? 0 : 1) | group total = count(), unparsed = sum(is_unparsed), first_seen = oldest(timestamp), last_seen = newest(timestamp) by parser | let normalised = total - unparsed | filter normalised > 0 | filter unparsed > 0 | let drift_ratio = unparsed / total | filter drift_ratio >= 0.05 | columns parser, total, unparsed, normalised, drift_ratio, first_seen, last_seen | sort -unparsed | limit 100
